workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml

This workflow processes BMAD-ANNO annotations by dispatching them according to their intent field. It is the ACTION counterpart to scan-feedback (which only READS). The intent field maps 1:1 to BMAD correct-course severity levels.

STORY STATUS AWARENESS — Annotations mean different things depending on when they are written: - PRE-IMPLEMENTATION (story_status = ready-for-dev): Annotations are for INTENT ALIGNMENT. Minor AC edits are safe because no code exists yet. Direct edit is appropriate. - POST-IMPLEMENTATION (story_status = in-progress / review / done): Annotations signal either: (a) A BUG found during UAT → action: fix_bug → Step 2a (add fix task, minor is OK) (b) Feature does NOT match requirements → needs re-discussion → MUST be at least moderate. scan_feedback.py auto-escalates post-impl change_ac from minor to moderate (intent_escalated=true). If you see intent_escalated, explain to the user WHY it was escalated before proceeding.

step.goal: Classify annotation by intent, scope, and story status
step.action: For each annotation to process:
1. Read the intent field: minor, moderate, or major
2. Read the scope field: story (default) or prd-section
3. Read the action field: change_ac, fix_bug, add_dev_note, rework_interaction, lock_decision, annotate_fr, erratum, upgrade_epic, question
4. Read the story_status field: ready-for-dev, in-progress, review, done, unknown
5. Check intent_escalated flag: if true, the intent was auto-escalated by scan_feedback.py because this is a post-implementation AC change. Inform the user before proceeding.
6. Route to the appropriate step based on intent + scope + action combination
Routing table:
- intent:minor + scope:story + action:fix_bug → Step 2a (direct edit, safe at any stage)
- intent:minor + scope:story + action:add_dev_note → Step 2a (direct edit, safe at any stage)
- intent:minor + scope:story + action:change_ac + story_status:ready-for-dev → Step 2a (pre-impl, safe)
- intent:minor + scope:story + action:change_ac + story_status:in-progress/review → AUTO-ESCALATE to moderate → Step 2b (post-impl AC change = feature mismatch, needs correct-course re-discussion)
- intent:moderate + scope:story → Step 2b (BMAD correct-course)
- intent:major + scope:story → Step 2c (correct-course + PM/Architect)
- scope:prd-section + action:lock_decision → Step 2d (PRD decision lock)
- scope:prd-section + action:erratum → Step 2e (PRD erratum)
- scope:prd-section + action:annotate_fr → Step 2f (Story draft from PRD)
- scope:prd-section + action:upgrade_epic → Step 2c (treat as major)
- action:question → Step 2g (TECH decision protocol)

step.goal: Minor: Direct Story edit (pre-implementation or bug fix)
step.action: This step is ONLY for:
- Pre-implementation AC edits (story_status = ready-for-dev)
- Bug fix references (action = fix_bug, safe at any stage)
- Dev note additions (action = add_dev_note, safe at any stage)
If a post-implementation change_ac reaches this step, HALT and escalate to Step 2b.
Open the target Story file
Based on the action field:
- change_ac: Replace the specified AC text with the new content from changes[]
- fix_bug: Add bug reference to Dev Notes + create fix task in Tasks section
- add_dev_note: Append content to Dev Notes section
After edit:
1. Append entry to "### Deviation Notes" with timestamp and annotation ID
2. Mark the annotation as processed (add "processed: true" + timestamp to callout)
3. Update sprint-status.yaml if story status changed
Prepare change description from the annotation's reason and details fields
Invoke the BMAD native correct-course workflow:
- Load {correct_course_workflow}/workflow.yaml
- Pass the change description as the triggering issue
- correct-course will generate a Sprint Change Proposal
- Output file: {default_output_file}
After correct-course completes:
1. Mark annotation as processed
2. Link the SCP to the originating annotation ID in Deviation Notes
Same as Step 2b but with additional escalation flag:
1. Invoke correct-course workflow (same as moderate)
2. Add "ESCALATION: Route to PM/Architect for review" header to the SCP
3. Flag in sprint-status.yaml: story status → "blocked-by-major-change"
4. Output warning to user: "This change may affect multiple Epics. Review the SCP before proceeding."
Read the target_decision field (D1-D14)
Read the choice field
Open {prd_decisions} and update the corresponding decision line:
- Change "- [ ] PENDING" to "- [x] Locked {date} — choice: {choice}"
Mark annotation as processed
Generate an erratum file at {errata_dir}/ERRATUM-{annotation-id}.md
Include: original PRD text reference, user correction, reason, date
Mark annotation as processed
Based on the target_fr field, generate a Story draft file
Output to {draft_dir}/DRAFT-{target_fr}.md
Use the create-story-ext template but mark status as "draft-pending-review"
Mark annotation as processed
Extract the question from the annotation
Generate a [DECISION-TECH:<annotation-id>] marker
Follow the OpenSpec decision protocol from .claude/rules/openspec-decision-protocol.md
Generate the copy-to-deep-research block for ChatGPT Deep Research
After all annotations are processed, output summary:
- How many annotations processed by category
- Which Story files were modified
- Any SCPs generated (moderate/major)
- Any erratum files created
- Any draft Story files generated
- Remaining unprocessed annotations (if user selected partial processing)
